title: 10 best herbal creams for uterine fibroids

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and sterols, which help reduce inflammation in the uterus and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.

Vasodilatory properties: Nettle cream's vasodilatory properties can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation to the uterus, which may help reduce fibroid size and alleviate pain and pressure caused by fibroids.

Prostaglandin inhibition: Stinging nettle contains compounds that inhibit prostaglandins, hormone-like substances involved in uterine contractions. By reducing prostaglandin levels, nettle cream may help relax the uterus and reduce cramping and heavy bleeding associated with uterine fibroids.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected area. By alleviating symptoms of fibroids, such as pain and heavy bleeding, the cream can provide relief and promote a healthier uterine environment. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to fibroid growth. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative for managing fibroids.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Urtica dioica, such as stachydrine, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the size and alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids like caffeic acid and ferulic acid in Urtica dioica have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, which can help prevent the growth of uterine fibroids and reduce their severity.

Sterols: Sterols, including beta-sitosterol, present in Urtica dioica have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and pro-apoptotic properties, which can help reduce the size and alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids by inducing cell death in fibroid cells.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce fibroid symptoms.
Roots: Roots contain iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fibroid cells and reduce their size.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has fallen for 30 minutes.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Infuse 100g of dried plant material in 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer to a clean container.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 20g of shea butter and 50g of the infused oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fibroid-related endometriosis, such as pain and heavy bleeding.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice cream have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to stabilize the uterine lining and reduce fibroid growth and severity.
Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogenic compounds in licorice cream may help to balance hormonal imbalances that contribute to fibroid development and growth, promoting a healthier uterus.

Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce fibroid-related pain and discomfort. By regulating hormonal balances and reducing inflammation, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may offer a natural and effective solution for women suffering from uterine fibroids.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Licopyranocoumarin (LPC): It exhibits anti-fibrotic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the size and severity of uterine fibroids.
Isoliquiritigenin (ISL): It has been shown to possess anti-angiogenic and anti-proliferative effects, which may help inhibit the growth and development of uterine fibroids.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and estrogen-modulating properties, which help alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids.
Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with uterine fibroids.
Barks: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help relax the uterine muscles and alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Extract the roots of glycyrrhiza glabra by digging and harvesting them in the early morning hours.
Dry the extracted roots at a temperature of 50 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered roots with a moisturizing base such as coconut oil and beeswax in a 70 to 30 ratio.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes and then pour it into jars for storage and use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Angelica's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as angelic acid and isoangelicin, help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with uterine fibroids, alleviating discomfort and pain.
Antispasmodic property: Angelica's antispasmodic compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, relax the smooth muscle in the uterus, reducing spasms and cramping caused by fibroid growths.

Hormone-regulating property: Angelica contains phytoestrogens that can help regulate estrogen levels, which may contribute to uterine fibroid growth. By balancing hormone levels, Angelica creams may help reduce fibroid size and alleviate symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica, also known as angelica, creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains ferulic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of fibroid cells and reduce their size. Additionally, angelica's ability to relax the uterine muscles and improve blood flow may help alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain associated with uterine fibroids. This natural remedy may provide relief and support for women suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Coumarins: Coumarins in Angelica archangelica creams may help reduce the size and alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids by inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and promoting cell apoptosis (cell death).

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, in Angelica archangelica creams may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Stems: The stems are used due to their high concentration of essential oils, which have been shown to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the collected angelica archangelica roots and leaves in a warm dark place for 2-3 weeks.
Make a herbal infusion by steeping 20 grams of dried angelica archangelica in 200ml of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the infusion and mix it with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture at 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes to create a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with uterine fibroids, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Inulin: A type of fructan found in Dandelion, inulin may help reduce estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids, as high estrogen levels are thought to contribute to fibroid growth.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain taraxasterol, a compound that may help reduce uterine fibroid growth.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 20g of dried taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to the oil mixture.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter are fully incorporated then pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids, including pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn cream's antioxidant properties protect the body from free radicals that can damage cells and contribute to the growth and development of uterine fibroids, promoting overall uterine health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its rich content of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins. remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried leaves and berries of crataegus monogyna at the end of the summer season.
Extract the bioactive compounds from the plant material using 200ml of ethanol 70% for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then filter the liquid through a paper filter.
Mix 30g of beeswax with 20g of candelilla wax and 10ml of jojoba oil.
Combine the wax mixture with 100ml of the plant extract and heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 40g of fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves by hand or with a clean tool at dawn.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours or until crisp.
Combine 20g of dried plant material with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl.
Infuse the mixture for 2 hours over low heat or until the oil has turned a light greenish color.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and let it cool completely.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried paeonia lactiflora root and 50g of dried paeonia lactiflora flowers from a reputable supplier.
Combine the dried root and flowers in a 1:1 ratio and infuse them in 500ml of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion and combine it with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture and stir continuously until the beeswax and shea butter have fully melted and incorporated into the infusion.

Strain the infusion and discard the solids then measure out 500 milliliters of distilled water.
Mix the water with 20 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Add 20 milliliters of jojoba oil and 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil to the melted wax mixture.

Mix the powdered cnidium monnieri with 20ml of coconut oil and 10ml of olive oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.

Whip the cooled mixture with 1ml of vitamin e oil until it reaches a creamy consistency suitable for application to the skin for 3 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Gather lonicera japonica dried flowers and roots in equal proportions weighing 50 grams each.
Combine the herbs with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed organic olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days at room temperature while shaking occasionally every other day.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl discarding the solids.
Whip the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of pure lanolin until it thickens to a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried turmeric rhizomes and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 50 grams of the powdered turmeric with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Add 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil, 5 milliliters of coconut oil, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil to the mixture, stirring until fully incorporated.
Continue heating the mixture for an additional 15 minutes, or until the beeswax has fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches 100°f, then pour into sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for uterine fibroids are topical creams made from plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids, a non-cancerous growth of the uterus that can cause heavy bleeding, pain, and discomfort. The best herbal creams for uterine fibroids include Vitex agnus-castus, Angelica sinensis, Ginkgo biloba, Urtica dioica, Trifolium pratense, Paeonia lactiflora, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ulmus rubra, Taraxacum officinale, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, uterine fibroids can lead to severe pain, heavy menstrual bleeding, and fatigue, negatively impacting daily life, causing missed work, social isolation, and emotional distress.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to uterine fibroids that are treated with herbal creams are ovarian cysts, endometriosis, and adenomyosis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like turmeric, ginger, and boswellia may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as pain, cramping, and heavy bleeding associated with these conditions. Additionally, herbs like chasteberry and evening primrose oil may help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to these disorders.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with uterine fibroids is a blend of Red Clover, Maca, and Black Cohosh. Red Clover cream helps to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, while Maca cream supports hormonal balance and uterine health. Black Cohosh cream, rich in phytoestrogens, helps to ease menstrual cramps and reduce fibroid growth. This synergistic blend may help alleviate symptoms and promote natural healing of uterine fibroids, promoting a healthier and more balanced uterine environment. Regular use may also support overall reproductive well-being.
